options.text: hook the reader, plant the initial situation, hide the seed of trouble
summarise the plot, name the author, thank the publisher
describe the weather in detail

exercises.explanation: Happer, planter, promettre — three jobs, often in one sentence.

exercises.prompt: Proust's seven-word opening proves that a hook…

options.text: can be a question the sentence refuses to answer — no explosion needed
must start with an action scene
needs at least a paragraph

exercises.explanation: A man goes to bed early. Why « longtemps »? Why tell us? You're already reading on.

exercises.prompt: What a good first sentence does to the reader.
exercises.template: Une bonne première phrase {{b1}} sans expliquer.
exercises.explanation: Happer — to snatch, to hook. La harpe is for the orchestra pit.

exercises.prompt: What tips the initial situation over.
exercises.template: L'élément {{b1}} fait basculer la situation initiale.
exercises.explanation: L'élément perturbateur — the inciting incident; the decorator only moves furniture.

exercises.prompt: The « indice de perturbation » technique presents…

options.text: a stable life with one detail promising the stability won't last
total chaos from the first line
a list of all future plot twists

exercises.explanation: Maupassant's clerk's wife « souffre » of her modest home — the necklace tragedy coiled in one verb.

exercises.prompt: The first-person narrator is intimate but… (adjective: one-eyed).
exercises.template: Le narrateur en « je » est intime mais {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Borgne — one-eyed: the « je » sees only what one head can see. The price of intimacy.

exercises.prompt: Match each narrative term to its meaning.

pairs.right: the narrator knows, the page doesn't say

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order: "Stability plus crack equals a reader who stays."
Listen to this incipit. Which technique is doing the work?
exercises.transcript: Or Madame Vey ne lavait jamais sa vitrine le mardi.

options.text: The hidden clue — one broken habit promising the stability will crack
A dramatic explosion
A flashback

exercises.explanation: A washed window doing the work of a thunderclap: l'indice de perturbation.

exercises.prompt: Read this examiner's note on two student incipits and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Copie A ouvre sur une explosion : immeuble en flammes, cris, sirènes. Tout est donné, rien n'est promis — au deuxième paragraphe, le lecteur n'a plus de question. Copie B ouvre sur un facteur qui, pour la première fois en vingt ans, dépasse la maison numéro 9 sans s'arrêter. C'est tout. Et c'est exactement assez : une habitude posée, une habitude rompue. La copie B a compris que l'incipit ne crie pas — il retient. Le lecteur tournera la page pour savoir ce que le facteur sait.
exercises.passageEn: Paper A opens on an explosion: building in flames, screams, sirens. Everything is given, nothing is promised — by the second paragraph the reader has no question left. Paper B opens on a postman who, for the first time in twenty years, walks past house number 9 without stopping. That's all. And it's exactly enough: a habit set, a habit broken. Paper B understood that the incipit doesn't shout — it withholds. The reader will turn the page to learn what the postman knows.

questions.prompt: Why does paper A's explosive opening fail?

options.text: Everything is given, nothing promised — the reader runs out of questions
Fires are forbidden in fiction
It is too short

questions.prompt: What makes paper B's postman work?

options.text: A twenty-year habit broken once — stability with a crack, and withheld knowledge
The detailed description of his uniform
The reader learns immediately what's in the mail

exercises.prompt: Flaubert dating his opening to the morning of 15 September 1840 shows…

options.text: ancrer dans une époque — a precise date welds a destiny to History
that novels require footnotes
that the author lacked imagination

exercises.explanation: The era is a character: anchor your hero to a date and History starts working for your plot.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Proust ouvre sur un homme qui se couche tôt : sept mots, aucune action, intrigue totale.

options.text: Proust opens on a man going to bed early: seven words, no action, total intrigue.
The hook isn't an explosion: it's a question the sentence refuses to answer.
Stability plus crack equals a reader who stays.
a novel
the opening
a plot

exercises.explanation: « Le roman s'est imposé en Europe au XVIIIe siècle. » — a novel.
